Lesson 1 -- Gabriela's Story Begins

Who Is
Gabriela?

A1+ -- 60 minutes -- Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Your Dream Starts Here

"In one word: what excites you most about Paris 2027?"
"What do you know about French culture?"
"What is your favorite series right now?"
Chapter 1: The Dream

Today's Mission

"By the end of today, you will introduce yourself in English -- name, age, city, hobby, and dream."
"Imagine: you land in Paris. Someone asks 'What's your name?' You answer..."
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Your Word Suitcase

Eight words you need to introduce yourself

Vocabulary

Words 1-4

What people call you
Name
What people call you
"My name is Gabriela."
Where your home is
Live
Where your home is
"I live in São Paulo."
A person who goes to school
Student
A person who goes to school
"I am a student."
Something you do for fun
Hobby
Something you do for fun
"My favorite hobby is watching series."
0 / 4 words revealed
Vocabulary

Words 5-8

To go to another country or city
Travel
To go to another country or city
"I want to travel to France."
Something you really want to do in the future
Dream
Something you really want to do in the future
"My dream is to visit Paris."
The one you like the most
Favorite
The one you like the most
"My favorite series is Gossip Girl."
To get new knowledge or skills
Learn
To get new knowledge or skills
"I want to learn English."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 3: The Code

Cracking the Code

The verb TO BE -- your first superpower

The Code

Look at the pattern

My name is Gabriela.
I am from São Paulo.
I am 16 years old.
She is a student.

What do the pink words have in common?

SubjectTo BeExample
IamI am Gabriela.
YouareYou are my friend.
He / She / ItisShe is a student.
We / TheyareThey are from Brazil.
The Code

Common Mistake

"I have 16 years."
"I am 16 years old."

In English, we use AM for age -- not HAVE!

The Code

Complete with am, is, are

I ___am from Brazil.
My name ___is Gabriela.
They ___are my friends.
She ___is a student.
I ___am 16 years old.
Chapter 4: Getting There

Meeting Sarah

February 2027 -- Charles de Gaulle Airport, Paris

Dialogue

Sarah meets Gabriela at the airport

SAR
Hi there! Are you from Brazil? I see your bag.
Comprehension

Did you understand?

1. Where is Sarah from?
Sarah is from Lyon, France.
2. Does Sarah like series?
Yes! She says "I love series too."
Listening

Close your eyes and listen

Gabriela introduces herself

0:000:00
Speed
1. What is her name?
Gabriela Pires.
2. Where is she from?
São Paulo, Brazil.
3. What is her dream?
To visit France in 2027.
Chapter 5: Practice

More Practice

Oral drilling, error correction, quick fire

Oral Drilling

I say Portuguese, you say English

1. "Diga seu nome em inglês."
"My name is Gabriela."
2. "Diga sua idade."
"I am 16 years old."
3. "Diga de onde você é."
"I am from São Paulo."
4. "Diga seu hobby favorito."
"My favorite hobby is watching series."
Oral Drilling

Keep going!

5. "Diga seu sonho."
"My dream is to visit France."
6. "Diga que você é estudante."
"I am a student."
7. "Diga sua série favorita."
"My favorite series is Gossip Girl."
8. "Faça sua apresentação completa em 30 segundos."
"Hi! My name is Gabriela. I am 16 and I am from São Paulo. My dream is Paris."
Spot the Error

Find and fix the mistake

"I have 16 years."
"I am 16 years old."
"My name Gabriela."
"My name is Gabriela."
"I student in school."
"I am a student at school."
"I from Brazil."
"I am from Brazil."
"My dream visit France."
"My dream is to visit France."

Quick Fire

Quick Fire Round

0 / 6
Someone at the airport asks: "What is your name?"
"My name is Gabriela."
1 / 6
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Your Turn

From guided to free -- show what you learned

Your Turn

Level 1: Guided

Read the model and fill in YOUR information:

"Hi! My name is ___. I am ___ years old. I am from ___. I am a ___. My favorite hobby is ___. My dream is to ___."
nameagecitystudenthobbydream
Your Turn

Level 2: Semi-free

No script! Introduce yourself using only these keywords:
name16São Pauloschoolhobbydream
Your Turn

Level 3: Free

Full simulation: Your teacher IS Sarah, the French girl at the airport. Shake hands, introduce yourself, have a real conversation. 3 minutes. No script. No help. Just you and English!
greetingintroductionquestionsanswersgoodbye
Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

Day 1 Complete

Survival card, checklist, and what comes next

Survival Card

5 Phrases to Survive

1
Hi! My name is Gabriela.
2
I am from São Paulo, Brazil.
3
I am 16 years old.
4
I am a student.
5
My dream is to visit France in 2027.
What I Learned

Lesson 1 Checklist

I can say my name, age, and city in English.
I know that "I AM 16" -- not "I have 16".
I can talk about my hobby and my dream.
I did a role-play with Sarah at the airport.
I know my 5 Survival Card phrases.

Stamp Earned!

Day 1 -- Complete

Lesson 2 -- Gabriela Arrives in Paris

Hello, Nice to
Meet You!

A1+ -- 60 minutes -- Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Welcome Back!

"Introduce yourself in 30 seconds. Go!"
"How was your homework? Did you record the audio?"
"If a French girl says 'Bonjour!' -- what do you answer?"
Chapter 1: The Dream

Today's Mission

"Today you will greet people, spell your name, and do a hotel check-in -- all in English."
"Imagine: you arrive at a small hotel in Montmartre, Paris..."
Chapter 2: Packing Words

New Words

Eight words for greetings and spelling

Vocabulary

Words 1-4

The first word you say when you meet someone
Hello
The first word you say when you meet someone
"Hello, my name is Gabriela."
What you say in the morning
Good morning
What you say in the morning
"Good morning! How are you?"
What you say when you meet someone for the first time
Nice to meet you
What you say when you meet someone for the first time
"Nice to meet you, Sarah!"
To say each letter of a word
Spell
To say each letter of a word
"Can you spell your name?"
0 / 4 words revealed
Vocabulary

Words 5-8

Your family name (Pires)
Last name
Your family name (Pires)
"My last name is Pires."
A greeting question (not a real question)
How are you
A greeting question (not a real question)
"How are you? -- I am fine, thank you."
When everything is OK
Fine
When everything is OK
"I am fine, thank you."
What you say when you leave
Goodbye
What you say when you leave
"Goodbye! Have a nice day."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 3: The Code

Greetings &
Spelling

The American alphabet is your passport

The Code

The American Alphabet

A = /ei/    E = /ii/    I = /ai/
G = /jii/    J = /jei/    R = /ar/

Spell your name: G-A-B-R-I-E-L-A

Now spell: P-I-R-E-S

The Code

Greeting Rules

Nice to meet you = FIRST time you meet someone
How are you? = a greeting, NOT a real question
Answer: "I am fine, thank you." (short!)
Time of DayGreeting
MorningGood morning!
AfternoonGood afternoon!
EveningGood evening!
Anytime (informal)Hi! / Hello!
LeavingGoodbye! / Bye! / See you!
Chapter 4: Getting There

Hotel Check-in

Montmartre, Paris -- February 2027

Dialogue

Gabriela checks in at Hotel Montmartre

REC
Good evening! Welcome to Hotel Montmartre. Can I have your name, please?
Comprehension

Did you understand?

1. What is the name of the hotel?
Hotel Montmartre.
2. How does Gabriela spell her last name?
P-I-R-E-S.
3. How does Gabriela feel?
Fine, a little tired, but very happy.
Listening

Close your eyes and listen

Hotel check-in conversation

0:000:00
Speed
1. What does the receptionist ask first?
Can I have your name?
2. How does Gabriela spell her name?
P-I-R-E-S.
3. Where is the hotel?
Montmartre.
Chapter 5: Practice

Time to Practice

Oral drilling, error correction, quick fire

Oral Drilling

I say Portuguese, you say English

1. "Cumprimente alguém de manha."
"Good morning! How are you?"
2. "Diga prazer em conhecer."
"Nice to meet you."
3. "Pergunte como soletra o nome."
"How do you spell your name?"
4. "Soletre seu sobrenome."
"P-I-R-E-S."
Oral Drilling

Keep going!

5. "Responda como você está."
"I am fine, thank you."
6. "Pergunte como soletra (educadamente)."
"Can you spell that, please?"
7. "Diga tchau educadamente."
"Goodbye! Have a nice day."
8. "Apresente-se com sobrenome para um recepcionista."
"Good evening. My name is Gabriela Pires. P-I-R-E-S."
Spot the Error

Find and fix the mistake

"Good morning, my name Gabriela."
"Good morning, my name is Gabriela."
"Nice meet you."
"Nice to meet you."
"How are you? -- Fine."
"How are you? -- I am fine, thank you."
"You can spell?"
"Can you spell that, please?"
"My last name is Pires. P-I-R-E-S." (letters in Portuguese)
"P-I-R-E-S" pronounced "pee-eye-arr-ee-ess"
0 / 5
Quick Fire

Quick Fire Round

0 / 6
You arrive at the hotel. Greet the receptionist.
"Good evening!"
1 / 6
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Your Turn

Hotel, airport, and cafe

Your Turn

Level 1: Guided -- Hotel

Hotel check-in. Your teacher is the receptionist. Follow the dialogue model on screen. Greet, give your name, spell it, answer "How are you?"
good eveningmy name isspellP-I-R-E-Sfine thank you
Your Turn

Level 2: Semi-free -- Airport

Airport check-in. No script. Greet the agent, give your name, spell your last name, answer questions. At least 5 exchanges.
greetingnamespellpassportthank you
Your Turn

Level 3: Free -- Cafe

You are at a Paris cafe. Greet the staff, introduce yourself, spell your name to reserve a table, ask the staff their name, spell it back. Say goodbye naturally. Total improv!
hellonice to meet youspellwhat is your namegoodbye
Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

Day 2 Complete

Survival card, checklist, and what comes next

Survival Card

5 Phrases to Survive

1
Hello! Nice to meet you.
2
How are you? -- I am fine, thank you.
3
My last name is Pires. P-I-R-E-S.
4
Can you spell that, please?
5
Goodbye! Have a nice day.
What I Learned

Lesson 2 Checklist

I can greet with Hello / Good morning / Good evening.
I can spell my name and last name in English.
I know the tricky letters: A, E, I, G, J, R.
I can say "Nice to meet you" and "How are you?".
I did a full hotel check-in in English!

Lesson 3: This Is My World

this / that / these / those + your school, friends, city

Day 2 -- Complete

Lesson 3 -- Gabriela Shows Her World

This Is My
World

A1+ -- 60 minutes -- Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Before we start...

"Introduce yourself in 30 seconds. Go!"
"Now spell your full name: G-A-B-R-I-E-L-A P-I-R-E-S"
Chapter 1: The Dream

Show and Tell

Pick 3 objects near you. Show the camera and say:

"This is my ___."
"This is my ___."
"This is my ___."

Examples: phone, pen, notebook, bag, water bottle

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Packing Words

People, places and things around you

Vocabulary

Words 1-4

Something close to you (one thing)
This
Something close to you (one thing)
"This is my phone."
Something far from you (one thing)
That
Something far from you (one thing)
"That is my school over there."
Close to you (more than one)
These
Close to you (more than one)
"These are my friends."
Far from you (more than one)
Those
Far from you (more than one)
"Those are the teachers."
0 / 4 words revealed
Vocabulary

Words 5-8

A person you like and spend time with
Friend
A person you like and spend time with
"This is my best friend."
Your parents, brothers, sisters
Family
Your parents, brothers, sisters
"I love my family."
A place where you study and learn
School
A place where you study and learn
"My school is on Paulista Avenue."
The area around your house
Neighborhood
The area around your house
"My neighborhood is quiet and safe."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 3: The Code

Cracking the Code

Demonstratives: this, that, these, those

The Code

Look at the pattern

This is my book. (one book, near me)
That is your book. (one book, far from me)
These are my books. (many books, near me)
Those are your books. (many books, far from me)

What do the pink words have in common?

Near (close to me)Far (over there)
Singular (1)this + isthat + is
Plural (2+)these + arethose + are
The Code

Common Mistake

"This are my books."
"These are my books."

THIS = singular (one) + IS   |   THESE = plural (many) + ARE

The Code

Complete with this, that, these, those

___This is my pen. (near, one)
___That is my school over there. (far, one)
___These are my friends. (near, many)
___Those are my old photos. (far, many)
___This is a good school. (near, one)
The Code

Articles: a, an, the

I am a student. (any student -- first mention)
I have an English class. (before vowel sound)
This is the library. (a specific one we know)
ArticleWhen to useExample
aOne (any) + consonant sounda friend, a school
anOne (any) + vowel soundan apple, an English class
theA specific one (we both know which)the library, the gym
Chapter 4: Getting There

Video Call with Helena

Helena from Lyon wants to see your school

Dialogue

Gabriela shows her school to Helena

HEL
Salut Gabriela! I am Helena from Lyon. Tell me about your school!
Comprehension

Did you understand?

1. Where is Helena from?
Helena is from Lyon, France.
2. What is Gabriela's favorite place at school?
The library.
3. Who are the students in red shirts?
They are Gabriela's friends from the music club.
Listening

Close your eyes and listen

Gabriela gives a school tour

0:000:00
Speed
1. What is Gabriela's favorite place?
The library.
2. Where are her friends from?
The music club.
3. What does she point to far away?
The cafeteria.
Artifact

My School ID Card

Gabriela Pires
Student ID: 2026-0342
SCHOOLAlumni High School
GRADE10th Grade
CITYSão Paulo, SP
YEAR2026
This student is enrolled at the school above.

Practice: "This is my school ID. My school is ___. I am in ___."

Chapter 5: Practice

Time to Practice

Oral drilling, error correction, quick fire

Oral Drilling

I say Portuguese, you say English

1. "Aponte para algo perto e diga o que e."
"This is my phone."
2. "Aponte para algo longe e diga o que e."
"That is my school."
3. "Fale sobre seus amigos aqui perto."
"These are my friends from school."
4. "Fale sobre pessoas longe."
"Those are my classmates."
Oral Drilling

Keep going!

5. "Apresente sua melhor amiga."
"This is my best friend, Helena."
6. "Fale sobre sua familia."
"This is my family. I love my family."
7. "Descreva seu bairro."
"This neighborhood has many cafes."
8. "Faça um mini tour da sua escola em 30 segundos."
"This is my classroom. That is the gym. These are my friends. Those are the teachers."
Spot the Error

Find and fix the mistake

"This are my books."
"These are my books."
"That are my friends."
"Those are my friends."
"These is my school."
"This is my school."
"I am a student in the school."
"I am a student at school."
"Those is my family."
"That is my family." (or "Those are my family members.")
0 / 5
Quick Fire

Quick Fire Round

0 / 6
You are holding your pen. Tell Helena what it is.
"This is my pen."
1 / 6

Level 1: Guided

Describe 5 things using this model:

"This is my ___." (something near you)
"That is ___." (something far)
"These are my ___." (near, plural)
"Those are ___." (far, plural)
"This is a/an ___." (with article)
thisthatthesethosea / an / the

thisthatthesethoseclassroomlibraryfriendsteachers

What I Learned

Lesson 3 Checklist

I know when to use THIS, THAT, THESE, THOSE.
I know that THIS/THAT + IS and THESE/THOSE + ARE.
I can describe my school, friends, and neighborhood.
I did a video call role-play with Helena.
I know my 5 Survival Card phrases.

Lesson 4 -- A Day in Gabriela's Life

My Daily
Life

A1+ -- 60 minutes -- Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Before we start...

"Describe 3 things near you using this, that, these, those."
"Tell me: What is your name? How old are you? Where are you from?"
Chapter 1: The Dream

Today's Goal

By the end of this class, you will describe your entire day in English.

From waking up...
...to going to school...
...to watching your favorite series at night.
Chapter 2: Packing Words

Packing Words

Actions and habits for your daily routine

Vocabulary

Words 1-4

To stop sleeping and open your eyes
Wake up
To stop sleeping and open your eyes
"I wake up at seven."
The first meal of the day
Have breakfast
The first meal of the day
"I have breakfast at home."
To travel to a place where you learn
Go to school
To travel to a place where you learn
"I go to school by bus."
To learn new things by reading or practicing
Study
To learn new things by reading or practicing
"I study English every day."
0 / 4 words revealed
Vocabulary

Words 5-8

To look at something on a screen
Watch
To look at something on a screen
"I watch series at night."
100% of the time, every single time
Always
100% of the time, every single time
"I always have breakfast."
Most of the time, but not always
Usually
Most of the time, but not always
"I usually take the metro."
0% of the time, not ever
Never
0% of the time, not ever
"I never skip breakfast."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 3: The Code

Cracking the Code

Present Simple: I wake up vs. She wakes up

The Code

Look at the verbs

I wake up at seven.
She wakes up at six.
I have breakfast at home.
She has breakfast at seven.

What do the pink words have in common when we use SHE?

SubjectVerbExample
I / You / We / Theybase formI wake up, They study
He / She / Itverb + SShe wakes up, He studies

Special: have → has  |  go → goes  |  watch → watches  |  study → studies

The Code

Common Mistake

"She wake up early."
"She wakes up early."
"He go to school."
"He goes to school."
The Code

Frequency Adverbs

Always100%
Usually80%
Sometimes50%
Never0%
PositionExample
Subject + adverb + verbI always wake up at seven.
Subject + adverb + verbShe never skips class.
Subject + adverb + verbThey usually study at night.

The adverb goes BEFORE the main verb.

The Code

Complete with the correct verb

She ___wakes up at six. (wake)
He ___goes to school by bus. (go)
She ___studies every day. (study)
He ___watches TV at night. (watch)
She ___has breakfast at seven. (have)
Chapter 4: Getting There

Video Call with Sarah

Sarah wants to know about your daily routine

Dialogue

Gabriela tells Sarah about her routine

SAR
Hi Gabriela! How are you?
Comprehension

Did you understand?

1. What time does Gabriela wake up?
She usually wakes up at seven.
2. How does she go to school?
She goes to school by metro.
3. What does she always do at night?
She always watches a series before sleeping.
Listening

Close your eyes and listen

Gabriela describes her daily routine

0:000:00
Speed
1. What time does Gabriela wake up?
Seven.
2. How does she go to school?
By metro.
3. What does she watch at night?
Gossip Girl or Friends.
Artifact

My Daily Schedule

Gabriela's Daily Schedule
São Paulo, Brazil
7:00Wake up + breakfast with family
7:30Go to school by metro
8:00Classes start
16:00Classes end + sometimes park
17:00Always do homework
19:00Study English
21:00Always watch a series
Routine Master -- Present Simple + Frequency Adverbs
Chapter 5: Practice

Time to Practice

Oral drilling, error correction, quick fire

Oral Drilling

I say Portuguese, you say English

1. "Diga a que horas você acorda."
"I wake up at seven."
2. "Diga que você sempre toma cafe."
"I always have breakfast."
3. "Diga como você vai pra escola."
"I go to school by metro."
4. "Diga que você geralmente estuda inglês a noite."
"I usually study English at night."
Oral Drilling

Keep going!

5. "Diga que sua irma acorda as oito."
"My sister wakes up at eight."
6. "Diga que você nunca pula o cafe da manha."
"I never skip breakfast."
7. "Diga que você sempre assiste series a noite."
"I always watch series at night."
8. "Descreva sua rotina inteira em 30 segundos."
"I wake up at seven. I have breakfast. I go to school by metro. I study until four. I usually study English at home. I always watch a series at night."
Spot the Error

Find and fix the mistake

"She wake up at seven."
"She wakes up at seven."
"He go to school by bus."
"He goes to school by bus."
"I have always breakfast."
"I always have breakfast."
"She study English every day."
"She studies English every day."
"He have breakfast at home."
"He has breakfast at home."
0 / 5
Quick Fire

Quick Fire Round

0 / 6
Your alarm rings at 7 AM. What do you do?
"I wake up at seven."
1 / 6

Three levels: guided, semi-free, free

Your Turn

Level 1: Guided

Describe your routine using this model:

"I always/usually/never ___."
"I wake up at ___."
"I have breakfast with ___."
"I go to school by ___."
"At night, I watch ___."
wake upbreakfastschoolstudywatchalways

No script! Tell Sarah about your full day using only these keywords:
wake upbreakfastschoolstudywatchseries

routinewake upalways / neverGossip Girlcompare

Day 4 Complete

Survival card, checklist, and your routine master stamp

Survival Card

5 Phrases to Survive

1
I always have breakfast with my family.
2
I go to school by metro every day.
3
I usually study English at home.
4
At night, I always watch Gossip Girl.
5
I never go to bed late on school nights.
What I Learned

Lesson 4 Checklist

I know the Present Simple with I/you/we/they.
I know the Present Simple with he/she/it (+S).
I can use always, usually, sometimes, never.
I can describe my daily routine in English.
I know my 5 Survival Card phrases.

Day 4 -- Complete

Lesson 5 -- Gabriela's Pop Culture World

Do You
Like It?

A1+ -- 60 minutes -- Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Before we start...

"Tell me your daily routine in 30 seconds."
"Use always, usually, sometimes, never."
Chapter 1: The Dream

Hot Takes!

Gossip Girl or Friends?
Pizza or sushi?
Instagram or TikTok?
Morning person or night owl?

Quick answers -- no rules yet!

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Packing Words

Feelings and pop culture vocabulary

Vocabulary

Words 1-4

The strongest positive feeling
Love
The strongest positive feeling about something
"I love Gossip Girl."
A positive feeling, but not as strong as love
Like
A positive feeling about something
"I like Friends."
A negative feeling -- you do not enjoy it
Don't like
A negative feeling -- you do not enjoy it
"I don't like horror movies."
The strongest negative feeling
Hate
The strongest negative feeling about something
"I hate Mondays."
0 / 4 words revealed
Vocabulary

Words 5-8

A TV show with many episodes and seasons
Series
A TV show with many episodes and seasons
"My favorite series is Gossip Girl."
A person inside a story, movie or series
Character
A person inside a story, movie or series
"Blair is my favorite character."
A very famous person that many people know
Celebrity
A very famous person that many people know
"Blake Lively is a famous celebrity."
When you cannot stop thinking about something
Obsessed with
When you cannot stop thinking about something
"I am obsessed with Gossip Girl."
0 / 4 words revealed
Chapter 3: The Code

Cracking the Code

Like, love, hate + noun or verb-ing

The Code

Look at the pattern

I love Gossip Girl.
I love watching Gossip Girl.
I hate horror movies.
I hate waking up early.

What comes after love / like / hate?

StructureExample
love / like / hate + nounI love pizza.
love / like / hate + verb-ingI love watching series.

Also works with: don't like, don't mind

The Code

Common Mistake

"I love watch Gossip Girl."
"I love watching Gossip Girl."
"She like pizza."
"She likes pizza."
The Code

Do you like...?

QuestionYesNo
Do you like...?Yes, I do.No, I don't.
Does she like...?Yes, she does.No, she doesn't.
Does he like...?Yes, he does.No, he doesn't.

Short answers: Yes, I do. / No, I don't. -- Never "Yes, I like."

The Code

Complete with the correct form

I love ___watching series at night. (watch)
She ___likes romantic comedies. (like)
___Do you like pizza? (do / does)
___Does he like reading? (do / does)
I hate ___waking up early. (wake)
Chapter 4: Getting There

Pop Culture Talk with Sarah

Gabriela tells Sarah about her favorites

Dialogue

Gabriela tells Sarah about her favorites

SAR
Gabriela, tell me about your favorites! What do you love?
Comprehension

Did you understand?

1. What series is Gabriela obsessed with?
She is obsessed with Gossip Girl.
2. What does Gabriela hate?
She hates horror movies.
3. Who is Gabriela's favorite celebrity?
Blake Lively is her favorite celebrity.
Listening

Close your eyes and listen

Gabriela talks about her likes and dislikes

0:000:00
Speed
1. Who is Gabriela obsessed with?
Blair.
2. What does she hate?
Horror movies.
3. What does she watch on YouTube?
Fashion videos.
Listening

Listen again

Sarah and Gabriela talk about series and food

0:000:00
Speed
1. Does Sarah like Gossip Girl?
She doesn't mind it.
2. What does Gabriela love?
Croissants and chocolate.
3. What series does Sarah prefer?
French series.
Artifact

My Favorites Card

Gabriela's Favorites
Pop Culture Fan Card
SeriesGossip Girl + Friends
CharacterBlair Waldorf
CelebrityBlake Lively
HobbyWatching YouTube + series
HatesHorror movies + Mondays
Pop Culture Fan -- Likes, Dislikes + Verb-ing
Chapter 5: Practice

Time to Practice

Oral drilling, error correction, quick fire

Oral Drilling

I say Portuguese, you say English

1. "Diga que você ama Gossip Girl."
"I love Gossip Girl."
2. "Diga que você gosta de assistir series."
"I like watching series."
3. "Diga que você odeia filmes de terror."
"I hate horror movies."
4. "Diga que você não gosta de acordar cedo."
"I don't like waking up early."
Oral Drilling

Keep going!

5. "Pergunte se eu gosto de Friends."
"Do you like Friends?"
6. "Diga que a Blair ama moda."
"Blair loves fashion."
7. "Pergunte se a Sarah gosta de K-pop."
"Does Sarah like K-pop?"
8. "Diga 3 coisas que você ama e 1 que você odeia."
"I love Gossip Girl, I love pizza, I love Blake Lively, and I hate Mondays."
Spot the Error

Find and fix the mistake

"I love watch Gossip Girl."
"I love watching Gossip Girl."
"She like pizza."
"She likes pizza."
"Does you like coffee?"
"Do you like coffee?"
"I hate wake up early."
"I hate waking up early."
"Yes, I like." (as a short answer)
"Yes, I do."
0 / 5
Quick Fire

Quick Fire Round

0 / 6
Your friend asks about your favorite series. What do you say?
"I love Gossip Girl. Blair is my favorite character."
1 / 6
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Your Turn

Three levels: guided, semi-free, free

Your Turn

Level 1: Guided

Talk about 3 favorites using this model:

"I love ___. It is my favorite ___."
"I like ___. I enjoy ___ -ing."
"I hate ___. I don't like ___ -ing."
lovelikehateseriescharactercelebrity
Your Turn

Level 2: Semi-free

No script! Talk about your likes and dislikes using only these keywords:
lovelikehateseriesmusicfood
Your Turn

Level 3: Free

Hot Takes debate! Your teacher is Marie, a French student. Marie disagrees with EVERYTHING you say. Defend your opinions about series, food, and music! Use love, like, hate, don't mind. 3 minutes. No script!
I love... because...I hate... because...Do you like...?defend your opinion
Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

Day 5 Complete

Survival card, checklist, and your pop culture fan stamp

Survival Card

5 Phrases to Survive

1
I love Gossip Girl. Blair is my favorite.
2
I like watching series at night.
3
I hate waking up early on Mondays.
4
Blake Lively is my favorite celebrity.
5
I am obsessed with Gossip Girl.
What I Learned

Lesson 5 Checklist

I can use love, like, don't like, and hate.
I know: love/like/hate + noun OR verb-ing.
I can ask "Do you like...?" and answer with short answers.
I talked about my favorites in the Hot Takes debate.
I know my 5 Survival Card phrases.

Pop Culture Fan Stamp!

Day 5: Do You Like It? -- Complete

Coming Up Next

Lesson 6: Numbers, Time & Dates

The tools you need to survive in Paris

Day 5 -- Complete

Chapter 1 — The Dream

Numbers, Time & Dates

The tools you need to survive in Paris

Warm-up

Quick Callback

Tell me one series you love and one you hate.
What is your favorite celebrity? Why?
Imagine: you are in Paris. What TIME is it right now in France?
Chapter 2

Packing Words

Your time and date vocabulary for Paris

Time Words

The exact hour — no minutes
O’clock
Used for exact hours
“It is three o’clock.”
15 minutes past or before
Quarter
15 minutes of an hour
“It is a quarter past two.”
30 minutes after the hour
Half past
30 minutes after an hour
“It is half past ten.”
12:00 PM — the sun is highest
Noon
12:00 in the daytime
“We have lunch at noon.”
0 / 4 words

Travel Words

12:00 AM — the darkest hour
Midnight
12:00 at night
“The flight arrives at midnight.”
It takes you from one country to another
Flight
A trip by airplane
“My flight is at nine o’clock.”
You buy this to enter a museum
Ticket
A pass to enter a place or travel
“How much is the ticket?”
How much something costs
Price
The amount of money something costs
“What is the price?”
0 / 4 words

Say It Right

O’clock
o’CLOCK
Quarter
QUARter
Half past
HAFPAST
Midnight
MIDnight
Ticket
TICket
February 3rd
FEBruary THIRD
Chapter 3

The Code

How to tell time like an American

Spot the Pattern

It is three o’clock.
It is a quarter past two.
It is half past ten.
It is a quarter to five.

What do the pink words have in common?

TimeSay
3:00It is three o’clock
3:15It is a quarter past three
3:30It is half past three
3:45It is a quarter to four
12:00 PMIt is noon
12:00 AMIt is midnight

Common Mistake

“Are three o’clock.”
“It is three o’clock.”
“3 February” (British/PT order)
“February 3rd” (American order)

Say the Time

7:00 → ???It is seven o’clock.
9:15 → ???It is a quarter past nine.
12:30 → ???It is half past twelve.
4:45 → ???It is a quarter to five.
12:00 AM → ???It is midnight.
12:00 PM → ???It is noon.
Chapter 4

Getting There

Gabriela at the Louvre ticket counter

At the Louvre

A
Agent: Good afternoon! Welcome to the Louvre. How can I help you?
G
Gabriela: One student ticket, please. How much is it?
A
Agent: It is 15 euros. Here is your ticket.
G
Gabriela: Thank you! What time does the museum close?
A
Agent: We close at six o’clock.
G
Gabriela: And what time is the next guided tour?
A
Agent: The next tour starts at a quarter past three. It is in English.
G
Gabriela: Perfect! Thank you very much!

Did You Catch That?

How much is the student ticket?
15 euros
What time does the museum close?
At six o’clock
What time is the next guided tour?
A quarter past three

Listen First

Close your eyes and listen. What times and dates do you hear?

0:000:00
Speed
When is Gabriela’s flight?
February 3rd, 2027
What time is the Eiffel Tower visit?
A quarter past four
How much is the Eiffel Tower ticket?
26 euros

Listen Again

A friend asks about the flight. Listen and answer.

What time does the flight depart?
Nine fifteen in the morning (9:15 AM)
What time does it arrive?
Eleven thirty at night (11:30 PM)

Gabriela’s Paris Itinerary

PARIS TRIP 2027 Gabriela Pires
Feb 3rdFlight departs: 9:15 AM Feb 3rdArrives Paris CDG: 11:30 PM Feb 4thLouvre Museum: 10:00 AM — Ticket: €15 Feb 4thLunch at café: 12:30 PM Feb 4thEiffel Tower: 4:15 PM — Ticket: €26 Feb 5thTrain to Versailles: 8:45 AM
Chapter 5

Practice

Numbers, time, and prices in action

Quick Fire

The clock shows 10:30. What time is it?

It is half past ten.

You want to know the time. What do you ask?

Excuse me, what time is it?

Your flight date is February 3rd. How do you say it?

My flight is on February 3rd.

The clock shows 2:15. What time is it?

It is a quarter past two.

You want to buy a museum ticket. What do you say?

One ticket, please. How much is it?

The clock shows 8:45. What time is it?

It is a quarter to nine.

1 / 6

Spot the Error

“Are three o’clock.”
“It is three o’clock.” — Always use IT IS
“My flight is on 3 February.”
“My flight is on February 3rd.” — American format: Month + Day
“It is quarter after two.”
“It is a quarter past two.” — Use A QUARTER PAST
“How much costs the ticket?”
“How much is the ticket?” or “How much does the ticket cost?”

Say the Time Fast!

3:00
7:15
10:30
4:45
12:00 PM
12:00 AM
Chapter 6

Your Turn

From guided to free — show what you learned

Role-Play: Guided

At the Louvre Ticket Counter
Teacher = Agent · Gabriela = Tourist

Buy a ticket, ask the price, and ask about tour times.

one ticket how much what time close guided tour thank you

Role-Play: Semi-Free

At the Eiffel Tower
Less help this time!

Buy a ticket, ask the price, ask when the last elevator goes up, and tell the agent your flight date.

February 3rd 26 euros

Role-Play: Free

At the Versailles Train Station
No keywords — you are on your own!

Buy a train ticket to Versailles. Ask what time the train departs, how much it costs, and tell the agent when you need to return.

Chapter 7

Wrap-Up

Your time survival kit for Paris

Survival Card

1
Excuse me, what time is it?
2
My flight is on February 3rd.
3
One ticket, please. How much is it?
4
The museum opens at ten o’clock.
5
It is half past twelve.

What I Learned

  • I can tell the time in English (o’clock, quarter past, half past, quarter to).
  • I can say dates in American English (February 3rd, 2027).
  • I can ask “How much is it?” and “What time is it?”
  • I can buy a ticket at a museum or monument in Paris.
  • I know the vocabulary: o’clock, quarter, half past, noon, midnight, flight, ticket, price.

Time Traveler Stamp!

Day 6: Numbers, Time & Dates — Complete

Coming Up Next

Lesson 7: Where Are You From?

Countries, nationalities, and the world around Gabriela

Day 6 — Complete

Chapter 1 — The Dream

Where Are You From?

Countries, nationalities & the world around Gabriela

Warm-up

Quick Callback

What time is it right now? Say it in English!
When is your flight to Paris?
Now imagine: you arrive at the hostel. Someone says “Hi!” — what is the FIRST thing they ask you?
Chapter 2

Packing Words

Your nationality vocabulary for the hostel

Identity Words

A nation — Brazil, France, Japan...
Country
A nation with borders and a government
“Brazil is a big country.”
Brazilian, French, American...
Nationality
The country where you were born or belong to
“What is your nationality?”
Someone from Brazil is...
Brazilian
From Brazil (same for he and she!)
“I am Brazilian.”
The nationality and language of France
French
From France; also the language spoken there
“She is French.”
0 / 4 words

World Words

Blake Lively and Jennifer Aniston are...
American
From the United States of America
“Blake Lively is American.”
Portuguese, English, French are...
Language
A system of communication (Portuguese, English...)
“I speak two languages.”
The document you show at the airport
Passport
An official travel document with your photo
“My passport is Brazilian.”
Many countries, many people = very...
International
Involving many countries or nationalities
“Paris is an international city.”
0 / 4 words

Say It Right

Country
COUNtry
Brazilian
braZILian
Nationality
nationALity
American
aMERican
Passport
PASSport
International
interNAtional
Chapter 3

The Code

How to talk about origin like a native

Spot the Pattern

I am from Brazil. I am Brazilian.
She is from France. She is French.
He is from Australia. He is Australian.
They are from Japan. They are Japanese.

What do the pink words have in common?

SubjectCountryNationality
Iam from Brazilam Brazilian
Youare from Franceare French
He / Sheis from Australiais Australian
Theyare from Japanare Japanese

Question: Where are you from? / What is your nationality?

Common Mistake

“I from Brazil.”
“I am from Brazil.” — Always use AM/IS/ARE
“I am Brazil.”
“I am Brazilian.” — Use the nationality, not the country name
“She is Francesa.” (mixing Portuguese)
“She is French.” — No gender change in English!

Say the Nationality

Blake Lively (USA) → ???She is American. She is from the USA.
Ed Westwick (England) → ???He is English. He is from England.
Chris Hemsworth (Australia) → ???He is Australian. He is from Australia.
Lily Collins (England) → ???She is English. She is from England.
Jennifer Aniston (USA) → ???She is American. She is from the USA.
Gabriela Pires (Brazil) → ???She is Brazilian. She is from Brazil.
Chapter 4

Getting There

Gabriela meets a traveler at the hostel

At the Hostel

G
Gabriela: Hi! I am Gabriela. I am from Brazil.
L
Liam: Hey! I am Liam. I am from Australia.
G
Gabriela: Nice to meet you! Where in Australia?
L
Liam: I am from Sydney. And you? Where in Brazil?
G
Gabriela: I am from Sao Paulo. It is a huge city!
L
Liam: Cool! What languages do you speak?
G
Gabriela: I speak Portuguese and I am learning English. What about you?
L
Liam: I speak English and a little bit of French.
G
Gabriela: That is awesome! How long are you in Paris?
L
Liam: Two weeks! This hostel is so international!

Did You Catch That?

Where is Liam from?
Australia (Sydney)
What languages does Liam speak?
English and a little bit of French
How long is Liam in Paris?
Two weeks

Listen First

Close your eyes and listen. What countries and nationalities do you hear?

Where is Liam from?
Australia
What nationality is Yuki?
Japanese
How many languages does Marco speak?
Three (Italian, English, and French)

Listen Again

Three travelers introduce themselves. Listen and answer.

Where is Sophie from?
Canada (she is Canadian)
What is Kenji’s nationality?
Japanese (he is from Japan)
What languages does Carlos speak?
Spanish and English

Gabriela’s Travel Profile Card

G
TRAVEL PROFILE
Youth Hostel Paris
NameGabriela Pires FromSão Paulo, Brazil NationalityBrazilian LanguagesPortuguese, English (learning) PassportBrazilian Age16 In Paris2 weeks (Feb 2027)
Fun fact: Paris hostels have travelers from 50+ countries!
Chapter 5

Practice

Countries, nationalities, and languages in action

Quick Fire

Someone asks: “Where are you from?” What do you say?

I am from Brazil. / I am from Sao Paulo, Brazil.

Blake Lively is from the USA. What is her nationality?

She is American.

You want to know where someone is from. What do you ask?

Where are you from?

Ed Westwick is from England. What is his nationality?

He is English.

You speak Portuguese and you are learning English. Say it!

I speak Portuguese and I am learning English.

Chris Hemsworth is from Australia. Complete: He is ___.

He is Australian.

1 / 6

Spot the Error

“I from Brazil.”
“I am from Brazil.” — Never forget AM/IS/ARE!
“I am Brazil.”
“I am Brazilian.” — Use the nationality, not the country!
“She is Americana.”
“She is American.” — No gender change in English!
“Where you from?”
“Where are you from?” — Questions need ARE!

Celebrity Nationalities

Say: “He/She is [nationality]. He/She is from [country].”

Jennifer Aniston
(Friends)
Ed Westwick
(Gossip Girl)
Chris Hemsworth
(Thor)
Lily Collins
(Emily in Paris)
Ryan Reynolds
(Deadpool)
Monica Bellucci
(Matrix, Bond)
Chapter 6

Your Turn

From guided to free — show what you learned

Role-Play: Guided

Hostel Lounge — Meeting Sophie
Teacher = Sophie (Canadian) · Gabriela = herself

Introduce yourself: say your name, where you are from, your nationality, and what languages you speak.

Gabriela Sao Paulo Brazilian Portuguese learning English

Role-Play: Semi-Free

Hostel Kitchen — Meeting Kenji
Less help this time!

Ask Kenji where he is from, his nationality, and what languages he speaks. Then introduce yourself.

where...from? languages

Role-Play: Free

Mystery Traveler at the Hostel Bar
No keywords — you are on your own!

A new traveler sits next to you. Find out: their name, country, nationality, and languages. Then introduce yourself completely.

Chapter 7

Wrap-Up

Your nationality survival kit for Paris

Survival Card

1
Where are you from?
2
I am from Sao Paulo, Brazil.
3
I am Brazilian. I speak Portuguese.
4
What is your nationality?
5
I speak Portuguese and I am learning English.

What I Learned

  • I can ask “Where are you from?” and answer about myself.
  • I can say my nationality and where I am from (I am Brazilian / I am from Brazil).
  • I can talk about what languages I speak.
  • I know the vocabulary: country, nationality, Brazilian, French, American, language, passport, international.
  • I can introduce myself to travelers at the hostel in Paris.

World Traveler Stamp!

Day 7: Countries, Nationalities & the World — Complete

Coming Up Next

Lesson 8: What Does It Look Like?

Describing people, places, and things around you

Day 7 — Complete

Chapter 1 — The Dream

Can You Do That?

Abilities, possibilities & asking for help in Paris

Warm-up

Quick Callback

Where are you from? What is your nationality?
What languages do you speak?
Now imagine: you are walking in Paris alone. You are lost. What do you DO?
Chapter 2

Packing Words

Survival vocabulary for asking for help

Survival Words

To say words out loud in a language
Speak
To talk, to say words in a language
“I can speak English.”
To know what someone means
Understand
To know the meaning of what someone says
“I can understand you.”
When you are lost, you ask someone to...
Help
To assist someone, to make things easier
“Can you help me?”
To say something again
Repeat
To say something one more time
“Can you repeat that, please?”
0 / 4 words

More Survival Words

To look for and discover something
Find
To locate something you are looking for
“I can’t find my hotel.”
To point at something for someone to see
Show
To point at or display something
“Can you show me on the map?”
The opposite of faster
Slower
Less fast, at a reduced speed
“Can you speak slower, please?”
A picture of streets and places
Map
A drawing that shows streets and locations
“Do you have a map?”
0 / 4 words

Say It Right

Speak
SPEAK
Understand
underSTAND
Repeat
rePEAT
Help
HELP
Slower
SLOWer
Map
MAP
Chapter 3

The Code

How to use CAN like a pro

Spot the Pattern

I can speak English.
I can’t find my hotel.
Can you help me?
Yes, I can. / No, I can’t.

What do the pink words have in common?

FormStructureExample
Affirmative (+)Subject + can + verbI can speak English.
Negative (−)Subject + can’t + verbI can’t find my hotel.
Question (?)Can + subject + verb?Can you help me?
Short answerYes, I can. / No, I can’t.—

Key: CAN + base verb (no “to”!). Same for all subjects (I/you/he/she/they).

Common Mistake

“I can to speak English.”
“I can speak English.” — No “to” after CAN!
“Can you speaking?”
“Can you speak?” — Base verb only, no -ing!

Can You Do It?

You / speak English → ???I can speak English. (Yes, I can!)
You / speak French → ???I can’t speak French. (No, I can’t.)
You / swim → ???I can swim. / I can’t swim.
You / find the hotel? (question) → ???Can you find the hotel?
She / cook (negative) → ???She can’t cook.
He / play guitar? (question) → ???Can he play guitar?
Chapter 4

Getting There

Gabriela is lost in Paris and needs help

Lost in Paris

G
Gabriela: Excuse me, can you help me?
P
Passerby: Yes, of course! What is the problem?
G
Gabriela: I am lost. I can’t find my hotel.
P
Passerby: What is the name of your hotel?
G
Gabriela: It is Hotel Le Marais. Can you show me on the map?
P
Passerby: Of course! It is here. You can walk. It is five minutes.
G
Gabriela: Can you repeat that? Slower, please.
P
Passerby: Yes! Walk straight. Turn left. Your hotel is on the right.
G
Gabriela: Thank you so much! I can find it now.
P
Passerby: You are welcome! Your English is very good!

Did You Catch That?

What is the name of Gabriela’s hotel?
Hotel Le Marais
Can the passerby help her?
Yes, he can! He shows her on the map.
How far is the hotel?
Five minutes walking

Listen First

Close your eyes and listen. What can Gabriela do? What can’t she do?

Can Gabriela speak French?
No, she can’t. She can speak English.
What can’t Gabriela find?
Her hotel
What does Gabriela ask the man to do?
Show her on the map and speak slower

Listen Again

Gabriela asks for help in three different situations. Listen and answer.

What can’t she find in Situation 1?
The Eiffel Tower
What can’t she understand in Situation 2?
The menu (at a restaurant)
What can Gabriela speak?
English

Gabriela’s Emergency Card

!
EMERGENCY CARD
Gabriela Pires — Paris 2027
NameGabriela Pires FromSão Paulo, Brazil NationalityBrazilian I can speakEnglish and Portuguese I can’t speakFrench
Emergency Phrases
1. Can you help me, please?
2. I can’t find my hotel.
3. Can you speak slower, please?
4. Can you show me on the map?
5. I can speak English.
Keep this card in your pocket — it can save your day!
Chapter 5

Practice

CAN and CAN’T in action

Quick Fire

You are lost. Ask someone for help.

Can you help me? / Excuse me, can you help me?

You cannot find the metro station. Tell someone.

I can’t find the metro station.

Someone speaks too fast. What do you say?

Can you speak slower, please?

You want someone to show you on the map. Ask!

Can you show me on the map?

Someone asks: “Can you speak French?” Answer no, but say you CAN speak English.

No, I can’t. But I can speak English.

You didn’t hear. Ask politely for the person to repeat.

Could you repeat that, please?

1 / 6

Spot the Error

“I can to speak English.”
“I can speak English.” — No “to” after CAN!
“Can you speaking English?”
“Can you speak English?” — Base verb, no -ing!
“She cans swim.”
“She can swim.” — CAN never changes (no -s)!
“You can help me?”
“Can you help me?” — CAN goes before the subject in questions!

CAN or CAN’T?

Listen carefully. Is it CAN or CAN’T? Click to check.

I ___ swim. CAN (I can swim.)
She ___ cook. CAN’T (She can’t cook.)
___ he play guitar? CAN (Can he play guitar?)
They ___ speak Japanese. CAN’T (They can’t speak Japanese.)
Chapter 6

Your Turn

From guided to free — show what you learned

Role-Play: Guided

Lost at the Metro Station
Teacher = passerby · Gabriela = herself

You are lost near the metro. Ask for help, say what you cannot find, and ask the person to speak slower.

Can you help me? I can’t find... slower, please show me

Role-Play: Semi-Free

Can’t Find the Hotel
Less help this time!

You walk into another hotel. Tell the receptionist you are lost, you can’t find YOUR hotel, and ask for help with the map.

help map

Role-Play: Free

General Emergency
No keywords — you are on your own!

Something went wrong in Paris (you choose what!). Find a stranger and use ALL your survival English to get help. Use can, can’t, repeat, slower, show, map.

Chapter 7

Wrap-Up

Your survival kit for Paris emergencies

Survival Card

1
Can you help me, please?
2
I can’t find my hotel.
3
Can you speak slower, please?
4
Can you show me on the map?
5
I can speak English.

What I Learned

  • I can ask for help using “Can you help me?”
  • I can say what I can and can’t do (I can speak English / I can’t find my hotel).
  • I can ask someone to speak slower or repeat.
  • I know the vocabulary: speak, understand, help, repeat, find, show, slower, map.
  • I can survive being lost in Paris using my emergency phrases.

Survival Hero Stamp!

Day 8: Abilities, Possibilities & Asking for Help — Complete

Coming Up Next

Lesson 9: Where Is It?

Directions & Getting Around Paris

Day 8 — Complete

LESSON 1
Teen English Program — Paris 2027

Gabriela Pires

From "tentar falar" to "pedir o caminho em Paris" — 48 aulas
São Paulo, SP Nível A1+ 15-16 anos — Estudante 3x/semana · 60 min
Blocos 1 + 2 — Aulas 1-5 e 21-25 0%
Velocidade do áudio:

Planejamento Pedagógico

Gabriela Pires — Teen English: Paris 2027 — 48 Aulas

Nome
Gabriela Pires
Idade
15-16 anos
Localização
São Paulo, SP
Profissão
Estudante do ensino médio
Foco
Comunicação oral + Paris 2027
Nível
A1+ (em transição para A2)
Frequência
3x/semana, 60 min, presencial
Evento-âncora
Viagem Europa — Fev/2027
Objetivo
Chegar à França e se virar em inglês em situações reais de turismo, com confiança e autonomia.
Distribuição
50% Speaking · 30% Listening · 15% Vocabulário · 5% Writing

Jornada do Personagem

Aluna que entende um pouco, tenta falar, mas ainda depende do português para quase tudo — e sabe que vai para a Europa em 2027.
↓
Viajante que chega à França, se vira em inglês em situações reais de turismo, conta histórias, opina sobre filmes e se apresenta com confiança para quem quiser ouvir.

De "tentar falar com apoio do português" a "pedir o caminho em Paris sem travar" — em 48 aulas com as séries, celebridades e viagens que você já ama.

Forças

  • Pronúncia com base fonética sólida — sem travas articulatórias
  • Disposição para tentar comunicar mesmo sem domínio completo
  • Alta tolerância ao erro — baixo filtro afetivo natural
  • Motivação ancorada em evento concreto (Paris 2027)
  • Alta energia e engajamento durante consultoria
  • 2+ horas de estudo fora das aulas — alto potencial de imersão

Pontos de Atenção

  • Forte dependência do português — barreira principal à fluência
  • Interlanguage em construção — sistema linguístico ainda frágil
  • Engajamento variável típico de adolescentes
  • Produção escrita praticamente não desenvolvida

Currículo — 48 Aulas

AulaTemaFoco LinguísticoAtividade PrincipalDever de Casa
01Who Is Gabriela? DiagnosticTo be + personal infoSelf-introduction baseline + Victory Wall poster60s baseline audio + Profile Card
02Hello, Nice to Meet You!To be + alphabet + greetingsHotel check-in role-playSpell name aloud + 8 to-be sentences
03This Is My Worldthis/that/these/those + articlesMap of my week + Gossip Girl scene5 photos with this/are sentences
04My Daily LifePresent simple + frequency adverbsRoutine vs. Rory Gilmore comparisonFull routine paragraph + 90s audio
05Do You Like It?Like/love/hate + Do you like…?Hot Takes game + French student role-playMy Favorites List + 60s audio
06Numbers, Time & DatesCardinals/ordinals + telling timeParis itinerary simulationDream Paris itinerary
07Where Are You From?To be + nationalitiesCelebrity Nationality Game + hostel role-play10 celebrity nationality sentences
08Can You Do That?Modal can + survival expressionsParis survival situations5+5 can/can't list + 90s audio
09Where Is It?Prepositions + directions imperativeFind the hotel map task + outdoor simNeighborhood map + directions audio
10What Do You Look Like?Descriptive adjectives + has/isGossip Girl character profile cardsCharacter description + 60s self-description
11Let's Eat!Would like + countable/uncountableCafé de Paris full restaurant simulationRestaurant dialogue + audio
12Shopping in the CityHow much + comparativesParis boutique shopping role-playShopping dialogue + 60s audio
13What Were You Doing?Past continuous + when/whileAlibi game + Gossip Girl retellingYesterday paragraph + 90s audio
14What Happened?Past simple regular/irregularWeekend story + verb wallWeekend story + 2min audio
15Have You Ever…?Present perfect + ever/neverBucket list activity + role-play10 ever questions + 90s audio
16Tell Me About Your Plans!Future going to + plansPlan your Paris week + phone call role-playFull Paris trip plan + 2min audio
17Emergency ModeHave/feel + symptoms + shouldPharmacy + emergency simulationsEmergency Card + 90s pharmacy audio
18Let's Compare!Comparatives + superlativesSP vs Paris vs NY + opinion debate10 comparison sentences + 90s audio
19Making Plans & InvitingWould you like + Let's + What aboutParis social weekend role-play3 invitation scenarios + 90s audio
20Block 1 ReviewAll structures recycledParis Survival Mega Role-playRe-record baseline + Vocabulary Book
21I Love My CityPresent simple + describing placesSão Paulo tour for SarahCity description audio
22Family & RelationshipsPossessive adjectivesFamily tree presentationFamily description + audio
23Telling StoriesPast simple narrativeTell my best memoryMemory story audio
24Series Marathon RecapPast simple + opinionEpisode review + recommendationEpisode recap audio
25Going ViralVocabulary social mediaTikTok script in English60s English TikTok-style audio
26School LifeVocabulary school + frequencySchool schedule presentationSchool week audio
27My Future SelfWill + future plansWhere will I be in 10 years?5-year vision audio
28Music & CultureLike + comparativesBrazilian vs international playlistMusic recommendation audio
29Travel StoriesPast simple + sequenceLast vacation full retellingVacation story audio
30Asking Better QuestionsWh-questions advancedInterview a celebrity simulation10 interview questions audio
31Dream BigWant to + would like toMy biggest dreams pitchDream pitch 90s audio
32Pop Culture Deep DiveRecycled vocab + opinionDefend favorite show debateOpinion piece audio
33Pronunciation Sprint ITH/R/L soundsMinimal pairs drill15 minimal pairs audio
34Texts & MessagesInformal EnglishWhatsApp in English challengeWhatsApp screenshots
35I Disagree, But PolitelyPolite disagreementFriendly debate role-playDebate audio
36If I Could Go AnywhereConditional 1Imagined trip planningIf audio (5 sentences)
37Career ConversationsJob vocabularyCareer interview simulationCareer story audio
38Pronunciation Sprint IIStress + intonationStress pattern drills10 stress sentences audio
39France 101Country vocabulary + cultureFrench culture quiz + roleplayParis facts audio
40Your Paris Survival KitAll survival vocab integratedFull Paris simulation: airport to dinnerSurvival recap audio
41Telling Jokes & StoriesPast + present mixedTell a funny storyJoke/story audio
42Real-Time ListeningListening strategiesWatch + summarizeSummary audio
43Speaking MarathonAll structuresTimed speaking tasksSelf-recorded marathon
44Networking with TeensSocial English advancedMeet new friend simulationFriend-making audio
45Defending Your OpinionOpinion + reasons + softenersMini debateDefense audio
46Storytelling for ImpactNarrative structurePersonal story 3minStory performance
47Final SimulationFull integrationParis arrival to departure simulationReflection audio
48Final Class — CelebrationCelebration + planFinal assessment + certificateContinue your English journey!

Metodologia

1. PPP (Presentation, Practice, Production)

Cada aula segue o ciclo PPP adaptado a 60 min: aquecimento, vocabulário com contexto, prática guiada, produção oral em pares e role-play livre.

2. Personalização Pop Culture

Conteúdo ancorado em Gossip Girl, Friends, Gilmore Girls, celebridades e a viagem para Paris 2027.

3. Speaking First

50% do tempo dedicado à produção oral. Gabriela aprende fazendo — não ouvindo explicações longas.

4. Cinestésico + Lúdico

Flashcards físicos, role-plays, simulações, objetos reais. Jogos de pattern drilling com variação de contexto.

5. Survival Card Progressivo

A cada aula, Gabriela ganha 5 frases novas para sobreviver em Paris. O cartão cresce até a viagem.

Mapa de Personalidade

Idade
15-16 anos — adolescente teen, peer-like
Stake
Chegar à Europa em fevereiro de 2027 sem conseguir se comunicar em situações básicas de viagem e perder a experiência por falta de autonomia linguística no momento em que mais importa.
Vitória
Sentir-se independente e segura na França — pedir o caminho, fazer uma compra, contar de onde é e por que está viajando, tudo em inglês, sem travar.
Citação-âncora
"Disposição para tentar se comunicar mesmo com apoio do português" — base do programa.
Estilo de Aprendizagem
Cinestésico + auditivo — aprende fazendo, praticando, repetindo em contexto real.
Energia Preferida
Dinâmica e variada — tédio é o maior risco. Atividades curtas, transições a cada 10-12 min.
Tolerância ao Erro
Alta — ri das próprias falhas e segue. Pode desafiar mais, com produção sem script.
Interesses Pop Culture
Gossip Girl, Friends, Gilmore Girls, Notting Hill, Blake Lively, fofoca leve, viagem.

Bem-vinda, Gabriela!

Seu programa Teen English começa aqui. Em 48 aulas, você vai transformar "tentar falar com apoio do português" em "pedir o caminho em Paris sem travar".

"Disposição para tentar se comunicar mesmo com apoio do português."

Frases de emergência (memorize antes da Aula 1)

I am sorry, I do not understand.
Desculpe, não entendi.
Could you speak more slowly, please?
Pode falar mais devagar, por favor?
How do you say that in English?
Como se diz isso em inglês?
Could you repeat that, please?
Pode repetir, por favor?
Wait a moment, please.
Um momento, por favor.
Aula 01 — Pre-class

Who Is Gabriela? — Diagnostic + Personal Introduction

Depois desta aula, você sabe se apresentar em inglês em qualquer lugar — escola, viagem, conhecendo gente nova.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de se apresentar em inglês — nome, idade, cidade, escola, hobby e sonho — em uma fala de 30 a 60 segundos com confiança.

Vocabulário Vocabulário

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

name·nome
“My name is Gabriela.”
live·morar
“I live in São Paulo.”
student·estudante
“I am a student.”
hobby·hobby / passatempo
“My favorite hobby is watching series.”
travel·viajar / viagem
“I want to travel to France.”
dream·sonho
“My dream is to visit Paris.”
favorite·favorito
“My favorite series is Gossip Girl.”
learn·aprender
“I want to learn English.”

V1. Match the words to their meanings Vocabulary

Selecione a tradução correta para cada palavra. Errou? Leia o exemplo de novo — é aí que se aprende.

name
live
student
hobby
travel
dream
favorite
learn

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas sobre a gramática.

Meeting at the Airport

Gabriela is a student from São Paulo. She is 16 years old. Her dream is to travel to France. Her best friend is Ana. Ana is also a student. They are classmates. Gabriela and Ana are excited about Paris. “I am so happy!” says Gabriela. “We are going to have an amazing time!” You are reading her story — and you are part of this journey too!

1. In the text, “She is 16 years old” — who is “She”?
A Ana
B Gabriela
C The teacher
2. “They are classmates” — “They” refers to:
A Gabriela and the teacher
B Gabriela and Ana
C Ana and you
3. Which form of the verb “to be” is used with “I”?
A is
B am
C are

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip GRAMMAR

Verb TO BE — Present Simple

Em inglês, usamos to be para dizer quem somos, de onde somos e nossa idade. Atenção: em português dizemos “eu tenho 16 anos”, mas em inglês é “I am 16 years old”.

PersonVerbExampleTradução
IamI am Gabriela.Eu sou a Gabriela.
YouareYou are my friend.Você é minha amiga.
He / She / ItisShe is from São Paulo.Ela é de São Paulo.
WeareWe are students.Nós somos estudantes.
TheyareThey are excited.Eles estão animados.

Negativo: I am not tired. She is not (isn't) from France. They are not (aren't) here.

Pergunta: Am I late? Is she a student? Are you from Brazil?

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Lê a dica em inglês com calma — é assim que se aprende de verdade.

"My is Gabriela Pires."
Hint: the word people use to call you
"I in São Paulo."
Hint: the verb that means to have your home in a place
"I am a at high school."
Hint: someone who learns at school
"My is to visit Paris in 2027."
Hint: something you really hope will happen
"I want to English to talk in France."
Hint: to get new knowledge or a new skill
"My series is Gossip Girl."
Hint: the one you love the most

P2. Choose the best answer Quiz

Clica na resposta. Verde = certo, vermelho = tente de novo. Errou? Lê tudo de novo com calma.

You meet a French girl in Paris in 2027. The best way to start is:
A "My is Gabriela."
B "Hi! My name is Gabriela. I am from Brazil."
C "Gabriela. Brazil. School."
D "I have 16 years."
Which sentence about your age is correct in English?
A "I have 16 years."
B "I am 16 year."
C "I am 16 years old."
D "My age have 16."

P3. Put this self-introduction in order Practice

Toque em cada item na ordem certa, ou use as setas para mover. Quando estiver pronta, clique em Check Order.

?"My dream is to travel to France in 2027!"
?"My favorite hobby is watching series like Gossip Girl."
?"I am 16 years old, and I am from São Paulo, Brazil."
?"Hi! My name is Gabriela Pires."
?"I am a student at high school."

P4. Read aloud Speaking

Ouça a frase, depois grave você falando. Use o Chrome — o sistema compara palavra por palavra.

Hi, my name is Gabriela. I am from São Paulo.
I am 16 years old and I am a student.
My dream is to travel to France in 2027.

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

Imagine you are at the airport in Paris in February 2027. A French girl your age sits next to you and says: "Hi! Where are you from?" How would you answer? Record your answer — there is no right or wrong here.
Sugestão de resposta
“Hi! I am Gabriela. I am from Brazil — from São Paulo. I am 16 years old and I am a student. This is my first time in France. What about you?”

Survival Card — Lesson 1: Who Is Gabriela?

1Hi! My name is Gabriela.Oi! Meu nome é Gabriela.
2I am from São Paulo, Brazil.Sou de São Paulo, Brasil.
3I am 16 years old.Tenho 16 anos.
4I am a student.Sou estudante.
5My dream is to visit France in 2027.Meu sonho é visitar a França em 2027.
Aula 02 — Pre-class

Hello, Nice to Meet You! — Greetings, Introductions & the Alphabet

Depois desta aula, você cumprimenta, soletra seu nome e sobrevive a um check-in de hotel em Paris.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de cumprimentar, soletrar nome e sobrenome e fazer um check-in de hotel simulado em inglês.

Vocabulário Vocabulário

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

hello·olá
“Hello! How are you?”
good morning·bom dia
“Good morning, teacher.”
nice to meet you·prazer em conhecer
“Nice to meet you, Sarah.”
spell·soletrar
“Can you spell your name?”
last name·sobrenome
“My last name is Pires.”
how are you·como você está
“How are you today?”
fine·bem
“I am fine, thank you.”
goodbye·tchau
“Goodbye! See you tomorrow.”

V1. Match the words to their meanings Vocabulary

Selecione a tradução correta para cada palavra. Errou? Leia o exemplo de novo — é aí que se aprende.

hello
good morning
nice to meet you
spell
last name
fine
how are you
goodbye

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas sobre a gramática.

Checking In at the Hotel

Gabriela arrives at the hotel in Paris. The receptionist says: “Good evening! Welcome!” Gabriela smiles and says: “Hello! My name is Gabriela Pires.” The receptionist asks: “Can you spell your last name?” Gabriela answers: “P-I-R-E-S.” The receptionist says: “Perfect. How are you today?” Gabriela replies: “I am fine, thank you!” Then she says: “Nice to meet you!” and goes to her room. “Goodbye!” she says with a smile.

1. What does the receptionist ask Gabriela to do with her last name?
A Write it
B Spell it
C Repeat it three times
2. When the receptionist asks “How are you?”, Gabriela says:
A “I have fine.”
B “I am fine, thank you!”
C “I fine.”
3. What does “Goodbye” mean in this context?
A Bom dia
B Com licença
C Tchau / Até logo

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip GRAMMAR

Greetings + Spelling Your Name

Em inglês, cumprimentamos de formas diferentes dependendo da hora do dia. Para soletrar, dizemos cada letra individualmente com a pronúncia americana.

SituaçãoEnglishTradução
ManhãGood morning!Bom dia!
TardeGood afternoon!Boa tarde!
NoiteGood evening!Boa noite! (chegando)
DespedidaGoodbye! / Bye!Tchau!
SoletrarP-I-R-E-S (pee-eye-arr-ee-ess)Essencial em hotéis e aeroportos

Pergunta útil: “Can you spell your name, please?” = “Pode soletrar seu nome, por favor?”

Resposta padrão: “How are you?” → “I am fine, thank you. And you?”

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Lê a dica em inglês com calma — é assim que se aprende de verdade.

" ! How are you today?"
Hint: a friendly word to start a conversation
"Good , teacher."
Hint: the part of the day before lunch
"Can you your last name, please?"
Hint: to say each letter of a word
"My name is Pires — P-I-R-E-S."
Hint: the family name that comes after your first name
"I am , thank you. And you?"
Hint: the standard polite answer to "How are you?"
"Nice to you, Sarah."
Hint: the verb you use when you see someone for the first time

P2. Choose the best answer Quiz

Clica na resposta. Verde = certo, vermelho = tente de novo. Errou? Lê tudo de novo com calma.

A hotel receptionist in Paris asks: "Can you spell your last name?" The best answer is:
A "My last name is Pires."
B "P-I-R-E-S."
C "Pires is my family."
D "I do not spell, sorry."
Which is the correct response to "Nice to meet you"?
A "Nice to meet you too."
B "Yes, please."
C "You're welcome."
D "I am fine."

P3. Put this hotel check-in dialogue in order Practice

Toque em cada item na ordem certa, ou use as setas para mover. Quando estiver pronta, clique em Check Order.

?"Good evening. My name is Gabriela Pires."
?"Perfect. Welcome to Paris, Gabriela!"
?"Yes — P-I-R-E-S."
?"Good evening! Can I have your name, please?"
?"Can you spell your last name?"

P4. Read aloud Speaking

Ouça a frase, depois grave você falando. Use o Chrome — o sistema compara palavra por palavra.

Hello! Nice to meet you. My name is Gabriela.
My last name is Pires. P-I-R-E-S.
How are you today? I am fine, thank you.

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

You arrive at a small hotel in Montmartre. The receptionist says: "Good evening! Welcome. Can I have your name?" How do you answer the full check-in conversation? Record everything you would say.
Sugestão de resposta
“Good evening! My name is Gabriela Pires. P-I-R-E-S. I have a reservation. Nice to meet you.”

Survival Card — Lesson 2: Hello, Nice to Meet You!

1Hello! Nice to meet you.Olá! Prazer em conhecer.
2How are you? — I am fine, thank you.Como você está? — Estou bem, obrigada.
3My last name is Pires. P-I-R-E-S.Meu sobrenome é Pires. P-I-R-E-S.
4Can you spell that, please?Pode soletrar, por favor?
5Goodbye! Have a nice day.Tchau! Tenha um bom dia.
Aula 03 — Pre-class

This Is My World — People, Places & Things Around Me

Depois desta aula, você apresenta seu mundo — sua escola, suas coisas, sua cidade — para qualquer pessoa em inglês.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de descrever pessoas, lugares e objetos do seu dia a dia usando this/that/these/those + artigos.

Vocabulário Vocabulário

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

this·este / esta
“This is my phone.”
that·aquele / aquela
“That is my school.”
these·estes / estas
“These are my books.”
those·aqueles / aquelas
“Those are my classmates.”
friend·amigo / amiga
“This is my best friend.”
family·família
“My family lives in São Paulo.”
school·escola
“My school is on Paulista Avenue.”
neighborhood·bairro
“My neighborhood is quiet.”

V1. Match the words to their meanings Vocabulary

Selecione a tradução correta para cada palavra. Errou? Leia o exemplo de novo — é aí que se aprende.

this
that
these
those
friend
family
school
neighborhood

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas sobre a gramática.

Gabriela’s World in São Paulo

Gabriela is showing photos to her friend Sarah on video call. “Look! This is my phone — I love it! And this is my cat, Luna.” She points to the window. “That is my school over there. And those are my friends in the park!” Sarah says: “Cool! What about these photos?” Gabriela laughs: “These are pictures of my family — my mom, my dad, and my brother. We live in a nice neighborhood in São Paulo.”

1. Gabriela uses “this” to talk about her phone. Is the phone near or far?
A Near — she is holding it
B Far — it is on a table
C We don’t know
2. “Those are my friends in the park.” Why does she use “those” and not “these”?
A Because there is only one friend
B Because they are far (in the park) and plural
C Because they are close to her
3. “These are pictures of my family.” The photos are:
A Far and singular
B Near and plural
C Far and plural

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip GRAMMAR

Demonstratives — This, That, These, Those

Em inglês, a regra é fixa: perto ou longe + singular ou plural. Em português é mais flexível, mas em inglês não pode trocar.

SingularPlural
Perto (near)this — This is my phone.these — These are my friends.
Longe (far)that — That is my school.those — Those are my books.

Dica: “this/these” = posso tocar. “that/those” = preciso apontar.

Pergunta: “What is this?” (perto) / “What is that?” (longe)

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Lê a dica em inglês com calma — é assim que se aprende de verdade.

" is my phone."
Hint: use this word for something near you
" is my school over there."
Hint: use this word for something far from you
" are my new sneakers."
Hint: the plural of "this"
"My best is called Helena."
Hint: a person you really like and trust
"My has four people."
Hint: your parents and siblings together
"My is in Vila Mariana."
Hint: the area of the city where you live

P2. Choose the best answer Quiz

Clica na resposta. Verde = certo, vermelho = tente de novo. Errou? Lê tudo de novo com calma.

You point to your phone in your hand. The correct word is:
A "That is my phone."
B "Those is my phone."
C "This is my phone."
D "These is my phone."
You point to two old photos far away on a shelf. The correct word is:
A "This is my photos."
B "Those are my photos."
C "That is my photos."
D "These are my photos."

P3. Put this "presenting my world" tour in order Practice

Toque em cada item na ordem certa, ou use as setas para mover. Quando estiver pronta, clique em Check Order.

?"That is my best friend Helena over there."
?"This is my school in São Paulo."
?"Those are my favorite books on the shelf."
?"These are my classmates in the picture."
?"This is my world — I love it."

P4. Read aloud Speaking

Ouça a frase, depois grave você falando. Use o Chrome — o sistema compara palavra por palavra.

This is my phone and that is my school.
These are my friends from school.
My family lives in São Paulo, Brazil.

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

A French exchange student is visiting your school in São Paulo. Show her around for one minute — what is your school, your classroom, your favorite spot? Use this, that, these, those.
Sugestão de resposta
“Hi! Welcome to my school. This is my classroom — these are my classmates. That is the cafeteria over there. Those are my friends from the music club. This is my favorite place — the library.”

Survival Card — Lesson 3: This Is My World

1This is my best friend.Esta é minha melhor amiga.
2That is my school over there.Aquela é minha escola ali.
3These are my classmates.Estes são meus colegas.
4My family lives in São Paulo.Minha família mora em São Paulo.
5My neighborhood is quiet and safe.Meu bairro é tranquilo e seguro.
Aula 04 — Pre-class

My Daily Life — Routines in São Paulo with the Present Simple

Depois desta aula, você conta seu dia inteiro em inglês — da hora que acorda até a hora que dorme.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de descrever sua rotina diária em inglês com 8 a 10 frases usando present simple e advérbios de frequência.

Vocabulário Vocabulário

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

wake up·acordar
“I wake up at seven.”
have breakfast·tomar café da manhã
“I have breakfast at home.”
go to school·ir para a escola
“I go to school by bus.”
study·estudar
“I study English every day.”
watch·assistir
“I watch series at night.”
always·sempre
“I always have breakfast.”
usually·geralmente
“I usually take the metro.”
never·nunca
“I never skip breakfast.”

V1. Match the words to their meanings Vocabulary

Selecione a tradução correta para cada palavra. Errou? Leia o exemplo de novo — é aí que se aprende.

wake up
have breakfast
go to school
study
watch
always
usually
never

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas sobre a gramática.

A Day in Gabriela’s Life

Gabriela wakes up at 6:30 AM every day. She takes a shower and has breakfast. Her mom makes coffee. Gabriela goes to school at 7:15 AM. She studies until 12:30 PM. After lunch, she watches Gossip Girl on her phone. In the evening, she does her homework. Her brother also studies at home. They have dinner together at 8 PM. Gabriela goes to bed at 10:30 PM.

1. “She takes a shower.” Why is there an “s” at the end of “take”?
A Because it is past tense
B Because “she” is 3rd person singular
C Because it is a question
2. “She watches Gossip Girl.” Why is it “watches” and not “watchs”?
A Verbs ending in -ch add “-es”, not just “-s”
B It is an irregular verb
C “Watchs” is also correct
3. “They have dinner together.” Why is it “have” and not “has”?
A Because it is a question
B Because “have” never changes
C Because “they” is plural — only he/she/it uses “has”

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip GRAMMAR

Present Simple — Daily Routines

Usamos o Present Simple para rotinas e hábitos. Na 3ª pessoa singular (he/she/it), o verbo ganha um -s (ou -es).

PersonExampleTradução
I / You / We / TheyI wake up at 6:30.Eu acordo às 6:30.
He / She / ItShe wakes up at 6:30.Ela acorda às 6:30.
Verbos em -ch, -sh, -ss, -oShe watches TV. He goes to school.Adicionam -es
have → hasShe has breakfast. (They have…)Irregular

Negativo: I don’t wake up early. She doesn’t watch TV. (o verbo volta sem -s!)

Pergunta: Do you wake up early? Does she watch TV? (o verbo volta sem -s!)

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Lê a dica em inglês com calma — é assim que se aprende de verdade.

"I up at seven o'clock."
Hint: to stop sleeping in the morning
"I have before school."
Hint: the first meal of the day
"I to school by metro."
Hint: to move from one place to another
"I English every day."
Hint: to read and practice to learn something
"I have breakfast — every single day."
Hint: 100 percent of the time
"I skip class — never, ever."
Hint: 0 percent of the time

P2. Choose the best answer Quiz

Clica na resposta. Verde = certo, vermelho = tente de novo. Errou? Lê tudo de novo com calma.

Which sentence uses third person -s correctly?
A "My sister wake up at six."
B "My sister wakes up at six."
C "My sister waking up at six."
D "My sister wake ups at six."
You skip breakfast 0% of the time. The best word is:
A "I sometimes skip breakfast."
B "I always skip breakfast."
C "I never skip breakfast."
D "I usually skip breakfast."

P3. Put this morning routine in order Practice

Toque em cada item na ordem certa, ou use as setas para mover. Quando estiver pronta, clique em Check Order.

?"I wake up at seven o'clock."
?"I study and have classes until four."
?"I have breakfast with my family."
?"At night, I always watch a series before sleeping."
?"I take the metro to school."

P4. Read aloud Speaking

Ouça a frase, depois grave você falando. Use o Chrome — o sistema compara palavra por palavra.

I wake up at seven and I have breakfast.
I always study English in the evening.
On weekdays, I never skip school.

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

A new friend in Paris asks: "What does a normal day in São Paulo look like for you?" Tell your full day — morning, afternoon, evening — with frequency adverbs.
Sugestão de resposta
“I usually wake up at seven. I always have breakfast with my family. I go to school by metro. I study until four in the afternoon. I usually study English at home. At night, I always watch a series — usually Gossip Girl or Friends.”

Survival Card — Lesson 4: My Daily Life

1I wake up at seven o'clock.Eu acordo às sete.
2I always have breakfast.Eu sempre tomo café da manhã.
3I go to school by metro.Vou para a escola de metrô.
4I usually study English at night.Geralmente estudo inglês à noite.
5I never skip class.Eu nunca falto à aula.
Aula 05 — Pre-class

Do You Like It? — Likes, Dislikes & Pop Culture

Depois desta aula, você fala das suas séries, seus crushes e suas opiniões em inglês como uma fã de verdade.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de expressar gostos, desgostos e opiniões sobre séries e celebridades em inglês com 5 a 8 frases.

Vocabulário Vocabulário

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

love·amar / adorar
“I love Gossip Girl.”
like·gostar
“I like Friends.”
don't like·não gostar
“I don't like horror movies.”
hate·odiar
“I hate Mondays.”
series·série
“My favorite series is Friends.”
character·personagem
“Blair is my favorite character.”
celebrity·celebridade
“Blake Lively is a famous celebrity.”
obsessed with·obcecada por
“I am obsessed with Gilmore Girls.”

V1. Match the words to their meanings Vocabulary

Selecione a tradução correta para cada palavra. Errou? Leia o exemplo de novo — é aí que se aprende.

love
like
don't like
hate
series
character
celebrity
obsessed with

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas sobre a gramática.

Gabriela’s Pop Culture Favorites

Gabriela loves watching series. Her favorite show is Gossip Girl. She likes Blair Waldorf because Blair is smart and stylish. She also loves listening to pop music — especially Taylor Swift. Her friend Ana hates horror movies, but Gabriela doesn’t mind them. “Do you like K-pop?” Ana asks. “Yes, I do! I love BTS!” says Gabriela. She doesn’t like waking up early, but she likes going to school to see her friends.

1. “Gabriela loves watching series.” What comes after “loves”?
A A verb in the past (watched)
B A verb with -ing (watching)
C A verb with -s (watches)
2. “She doesn’t like waking up early.” This means:
A Ela ama acordar cedo
B Ela não gosta de acordar cedo
C Ela odeia a escola
3. Ana asks: “Do you like K-pop?” The correct short answer is:
A “Yes, I like.”
B “Yes, I do!”
C “Yes, I am.”

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip GRAMMAR

Like / Love / Hate + Noun ou Verb-ing

Em inglês, depois de like, love, hate, don’t mind usamos um substantivo OU verbo com -ing. Nunca o infinitivo com “to” nesse contexto informal.

EstruturaExampleTradução
love + nounI love series.Amo séries.
love + verb-ingI love watching Gossip Girl.Amo assistir Gossip Girl.
like + verb-ingShe likes going to school.Ela gosta de ir à escola.
hate + nounAna hates horror movies.Ana odeia filmes de terror.
don’t mind + nounI don’t mind them.Não me importo com eles.

Pergunta: “Do you like K-pop?” → “Yes, I do!” / “No, I don’t.”

Atenção: Na 3ª pessoa: “Does she like series?” → “Yes, she does.”

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Lê a dica em inglês com calma — é assim que se aprende de verdade.

"I Gossip Girl — it is my favorite."
Hint: a strong word for liking something a lot
"I don't horror movies — they scare me."
Hint: the verb for enjoying something a normal amount
"I Mondays — they are so boring."
Hint: a strong word for not liking at all
"My favorite is Friends."
Hint: a TV show with many episodes and seasons
"Blair is my favorite in Gossip Girl."
Hint: a person inside a story or series
"I am with Gilmore Girls — I watched it three times!"
Hint: when you cannot stop thinking about something

P2. Choose the best answer Quiz

Clica na resposta. Verde = certo, vermelho = tente de novo. Errou? Lê tudo de novo com calma.

You watch Friends every single day and you cannot stop. The best sentence is:
A "I don't like Friends."
B "I am obsessed with Friends."
C "I hate Friends."
D "Friends is okay."
Someone asks "Do you like horror movies?" You hate them. The best answer is:
A "Yes, I like."
B "No, I don't like."
C "No, I don't like horror movies."
D "I am not horror movies."

P3. Put this fan-talk in order Practice

Toque em cada item na ordem certa, ou use as setas para mover. Quando estiver pronta, clique em Check Order.

?"I am obsessed with Blair Waldorf — she is so smart."
?"What about you? What do you like to watch?"
?"My favorite series is Gossip Girl."
?"I don't like Chuck Bass in season one — he is mean."
?"I also love Friends because it makes me laugh."

P4. Read aloud Speaking

Ouça a frase, depois grave você falando. Use o Chrome — o sistema compara palavra por palavra.

I love Gossip Girl. Blair is my favorite character.
I do not like horror movies. I prefer comedies.
I am obsessed with Friends. I have seen it many times.

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.
